items.headline: Two Australian states waive public transport fares as fuel costs surge
items.body: Victoria made public transport free for one month starting March 31, while Tasmania removed fares for buses and ferries from March 30 to July 1. Federal government halved fuel excise and paused road-user charges for trucks for three months, reducing fuel costs by 26.3 cents per litre.

items.headline: Philippines becomes first nation to declare national energy emergency over fuel crisis
items.body: President Marcos signed an executive order putting the country under state of national energy emergency, as the Philippines imports 98% of its oil from the Middle East and was severely affected by the Strait of Hormuz crisis. The country has 45 days of oil supply, down from 55 days when the war started, with diesel exceeding 130 pesos per liter and gasoline surpassing 100 pesos.

items.headline: Japanese authorities signal intervention as yen hits critical 160 threshold
items.body: Japan's top currency official delivered his strongest warning after the yen crossed 160 per dollar, saying authorities may need to take bold action if speculative activity in the foreign exchange market continues. The yen has lost over 2% in value against the dollar since the war started, making it one of the worst-performing major currencies in the last month due to Japan's heavy dependence on energy imports.

items.headline: Olympics Committee restricts women's events to biological females starting 2028
items.body: The IOC reached consensus that male sex provides a performance advantage in all sports relying on strength, power and endurance, and that eligibility should be based on biological sex to ensure fairness and protect safety. All athletes wanting to participate in women's events must undergo genetic testing, with the policy taking effect for the 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles.
